hero.headline: Amity Online MBA in International Business Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. NAAC A+, NIRF #22. QS ranked. WES recognised.

sections.tldr: Amity University Online MBA in International Business Management suits professionals in export-import, global supply chain, or cross-border trade roles. NAAC A+, NIRF #22 at Rs 1,99,000. WES recognition supports international credential verification. Sem 3-4 spec syllabus confirmed each cycle.

about.body:
International Business Management is an MBA specialisation built for professionals working at the intersection of global trade, multinational operations, cross-border supply chains, and foreign investment. The Amity Online MBA in International Business Management covers international trade regulations, foreign exchange management, global marketing, cross-cultural management, and trade finance.

The specialisation suits professionals in export houses, logistics and freight forwarding companies, MNC subsidiaries, international procurement teams, and diplomatic trade bodies. It is also sought by candidates preparing for IIFT-type competitive examinations and those targeting foreign postings within Indian corporates.

Amity University Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #22 University ranking for 2025. The WES recognition is particularly relevant for International Business graduates who may work in or migrate to Canada, the US, or the UK — WES validates the Amity degree for employers and educational institutions abroad. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from Amity. Full programme overview at /universities/amity-university-online/mba.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Amity MBA specialisations. See the main Amity MBA page for core subjects.
syllabus.emptyFallback:
Semesters 1 and 2 of the Amity Online MBA cover the management core: Accounting for Managers, Managerial Economics, Marketing Management, Statistics for Management, Professional Communication, Business Research Methods, Financial Management, Human Resource Management, Legal Aspects of Business, and Conflict Resolution and Management.

Semesters 3 and 4 cover the International Business Management specialisation subjects. These typically include areas such as international trade policy and WTO regulations, foreign exchange management, global supply chain management, international marketing, cross-cultural management, trade finance and documentation, bilateral and multilateral trade agreements, and international business law. The specific Sem 3 and Sem 4 subjects for International Business Management are confirmed by Amity each admission cycle. Our counsellor can share the current spec-level syllabus within 24 hours.

whoHires.body:
International Business Management MBA graduates from Amity are hired by export-import companies, MNC India subsidiaries, freight and logistics firms, government trade bodies, international banks, and consulting firms with global practices. Entry-level roles include: Export Executive, Import Documentation Specialist, Trade Finance Analyst, Global Procurement Analyst, and International Business Development Executive.

Mid-level roles include: Export Manager, International Business Manager, Global Supply Chain Manager, Trade Finance Manager, and Country Manager for South Asian operations. Senior professionals reach titles such as VP International Business, Global Head of Trade, and Director of International Operations.

Key hiring organisations include Tata International, Mahindra International, export-heavy manufacturing companies, large freight forwarders (DHL, Maersk, Kuehne+Nagel), EXIM Bank, major private and public sector banks with trade finance desks, and the Indian Trade Promotion Organisation (ITPO). WES recognition is particularly valued by candidates targeting positions with international organisations or relocating to Canada or the UK.

skills.body:
The International Business Management specialisation builds skills directly applicable to cross-border trade and multinational management roles:

International Trade Law and WTO Regulations: understanding tariff structures, trade agreements (RCEP, CPTPP, bilateral FTAs), anti-dumping regulations, and WTO dispute settlement — tested in export management and trade compliance roles. Foreign Exchange Management: forex risk, hedging instruments, FEMA compliance, and RBI guidelines relevant to treasury and trade finance functions. Trade Finance and Documentation: letters of credit, bank guarantees, export documentation, Incoterms, and customs procedures — core knowledge for export executive and trade finance analyst roles. Cross-Cultural Management: managing diverse international teams, negotiation styles across cultures, and global communication — increasingly relevant in MNC management roles. Global Supply Chain: sourcing strategy, global logistics coordination, international vendor management, and supply chain risk in global operations contexts.

Analytical skills include trade data interpretation, market entry analysis, and competitive landscape mapping across international markets.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amity Online MBA International Business against peers:

Amity vs NMIMS Online MBA (/universities/nmims-online/mba): NMIMS does not offer a dedicated International Business specialisation in its online programme. Amity's focused IB spec with trade law and forex content is a differentiator for candidates targeting EXIM roles. NMIMS carries stronger overall brand but lacks this specific track.

Amity vs Symbiosis Online MBA (/universities/symbiosis-university-online/mba): Symbiosis offers International Business as a specialisation and carries a strong brand in Pune and western India. Symbiosis IB graduates are well regarded at MNC procurement and logistics companies. Fees are higher. For candidates outside Pune and Maharashtra targeting pan-India or global roles, Amity at Rs 1,99,000 is competitive.

Amity vs Manipal University Jaipur Online MBA (/universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba): Manipal Jaipur (MAHE group) has WES recognition and a decent international business module within its General Management track. It does not offer a dedicated IB spec. Amity's dedicated IB specialisation is more appropriate for candidates specifically targeting international trade careers.

fees.body: The total fee for the Amity University Online MBA (all specialisations including International Business Management) is approximately Rs 1,99,000. This includes a one-time registration fee of Rs 2,500 and four semester fees of Rs 49,750 each. EMI options start from Rs 8,292 per month. Examination fees may apply separately. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees each quarter. Always verify the current fee at the official Amity Online portal before making any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Amity University degree certificate carries the Amity University name and the MBA designation. The specialisation (International Business Management) is noted on the marksheet and transcript. The certificate does not display the word online on the face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms. The degree is WES recognised, meaning it can be officially verified for employers and educational institutions in Canada, the US, and the UK. Request a sample certificate via /contact.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amity Online MBA International Business alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Arjun Malhotra
items.liked: Trade finance and Incoterms module was directly useful for my export documentation work
items.disliked: WTO regulations content was theoretical — needed more India-specific FTA case studies
items.body: I work in an export house and the LC documentation and Incoterms content was immediately applicable. Within a month of that module I had restructured our trade documentation process. The Amity name combined with the IB spec helped me negotiate a promotion to Export Manager. WES recognition was the deciding factor in choosing Amity over cheaper alternatives.

items.name: Sonali Kapoor
items.liked: Forex management content covered FEMA compliance clearly
items.disliked: Cross-cultural management section was too textbook — needed more current multinational examples
items.body: The forex and treasury content was the strongest part of this spec. I use FEMA compliance knowledge in my current treasury role at an import-heavy manufacturing company. The Amity degree helped me clear the CV screening for roles that required a management degree. Cross-cultural content was decent but not as practically oriented as the finance modules.

items.name: Karan Singh
items.liked: WES recognition was confirmed and employer in Canada accepted the degree
items.disliked: Global supply chain content needed more digital freight and 3PL examples
items.body: I was planning to move to Canada for work after completing the degree. WES recognition was the primary reason I chose Amity IB. The recognition was smooth — WES evaluation confirmed the degree without issues. My Canadian employer had no concerns. Global supply chain content was decent but a bit dated on logistics technology.

faqs.question: Is Amity Online MBA International Business suitable for IIFT exam preparation?
faqs.answer: The MBA curriculum shares some conceptual overlap with IIFT syllabi on international trade, forex, and trade policy. However, the MBA is not a preparation programme for the IIFT entrance examination. Candidates seeking IIFT admission should pursue dedicated exam coaching. The Amity IB MBA is a post-graduate degree, not an exam prep track.

faqs.question: Does WES recognition help with working in Gulf countries as well as Canada?
faqs.answer: WES primarily evaluates credentials for Canada, the US, and some Australian institutions. Gulf countries typically rely on equivalency certificates from national bodies (eg. UAE's Ministry of Education). Amity's NAAC A+ status and UGC-DEB approval are the relevant credentials for Gulf employers. Confirm country-specific requirements with the relevant embassy or employer before relying on WES for Gulf applications.

faqs.question: What is the salary range for International Business MBA graduates?
faqs.answer: Export executives and trade finance analysts at entry level typically see Rs 4-7 lakh packages. International Business Managers with 3-5 years experience see Rs 10-18 lakh at MNC subsidiaries and large trading companies. Salary varies significantly by sector — logistics and freight forwarding typically pay lower than banking trade finance or MNC procurement.

faqs.question: Does Amity Online MBA International Business cover digital trade and e-commerce exports?
faqs.answer: The core curriculum may not cover e-commerce exports and digital trade protocols in depth, as these are recent additions to trade law. Confirm with Amity whether the current Sem 3-4 syllabus includes DPIIT export schemes, ONDC, or cross-border e-commerce regulations. Our counsellor can clarify current module coverage.

faqs.question: Can I enrol in International Business Management if I have no export or trade experience?
faqs.answer: Yes. No prior trade experience is required. The Sem 1-2 core builds management fundamentals including macroeconomics and legal aspects of business. Sem 3-4 builds the IB specialisation from that base. Professionals without trade experience benefit most from supplementing the degree with export documentation certifications and an entry-level export executive role.

chooseIf: You are in an export house, freight forwarding company, or MNC subsidiary and want a NIRF-ranked formal IB qualification.
You are planning to work or migrate to Canada or the UK and need WES-recognisable credentials.
You want a dedicated International Business specialisation covering trade law, forex, and global supply chain in a single credential.
consider: You are targeting premium MNC management roles — Symbiosis Online MBA International Business carries stronger brand at those companies.
You want a broader management degree without committing to IB specialisation — General Management or Finance may offer wider career optionality.
You are targeting IIFT or ISB admission — those are entrance-exam based full-time programmes, not comparable to an online MBA.
